name: Kaleb Johnson
headline: Buried rookie must show more before fantasy matters
body:
Johnson's rookie tape was a cautionary file: just 28 carries for 69 yards across 10 games, a brutal 2.46 per attempt and a -0.249 EPA per play that ranked among the worst at the position. His 9.1% snap share and zero rushing scores tell the story of a back the staff barely trusted, and the underlying contact numbers offer little hope of a hidden gem, with only 1.21 yards after contact per attempt and two broken tackles all year.

The path forward is just as crowded. He sits behind Jaylen Warren and Rico Dowdle in a three-man committee, projecting as no better than an RB3 with negligible standalone value in standard leagues. The 2024 college profile (1,537 yards, 21 touchdowns, 22 catches) hints at upside if injuries open a lane, but until he earns real volume he is a dynasty stash and watch-list name, not a draftable asset.
